summary: In the hand-authored my-queue command, tickets without a `due_by` value now sort to the bottom and render their MINUTES column as `—` instead of `0`. Sort is now stable secondary on minutes-remaining.
reason: Greptile noted in PR #533 round-2 review (not elevated to an inline finding, but mentioned in the Important Files Changed remark): tickets with no due_by kept MinutesTo at its float64 zero, so they floated to the top of my-queue as if due-right-now. This is hand-authored code in freshservice_novel.go, not generator output — fix lives only in the printed CLI.
validated_outcome: `my-queue` now lists SLA-tracked tickets first (sorted by minutes-to-breach) and no-SLA tickets after, with the MINUTES column showing `—` rather than `0` for the latter.
